title: Global ops skills should separate workflow from reference material
rule_summary: For broad operational skills, keep `SKILL.md` focused on workflow, priorities, and output shape; place command-heavy material in `references/` files.
lesson_summary: Operational skills become more reusable when the main file contains decision flow and durable rules, while command catalogs and long checklists live in references that are loaded only when needed.

title: Add DeepSeek summarization to superroo-learn.mjs for cross-project lesson extraction
rule_summary: Any CLI tool that auto-extracts lessons from git commits should use AI summarization (DeepSeek API) to generate structured, reusable lesson content instead of storing raw commit messages. Always implement graceful fallback to raw content when the API is unavailable.
lesson_summary: Added DeepSeek API summarization to the cross-project learning layer CLI (superroo-learn.mjs). Previously, cmdExtractCommit() and cmdScan() stored raw commit messages as lessons without any summarization. Now both functions call deepseekSummarize() to generate a concise, structured summary of each commit before storing it in Central Brain (or local fallback). Graceful degradation: if DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is not set or API call fails, falls back to raw commit content.

title: Production deploys must keep source and dependency manifests in sync
rule_summary: When production source files change together with dependencies, deploy the matching `package.json` and verify generated runtime artifacts exist before declaring recovery complete.
lesson_summary: Hotfixes can restore one failing layer while leaving another stale layer broken. For production recovery, validate the runtime artifact set as well as the source change, especially when a deploy spans both app code and dependency manifests.

title: Safe JSON Parsing in Database Registries
rule_summary: All registry modules MUST use safeJsonParse() instead of raw JSON.parse() when reading from database.
lesson_summary: Always use safe JSON parsing with fallback values when reading from persistent storage. Database corruption can happen at any time; code should be resilient.

title: Tensor Division by Zero Protection
rule_summary: All Tensor mathematical operations MUST validate for edge cases (division by zero, sqrt of negative, log of non-positive) and either throw or clamp to safe values.
lesson_summary: ML operations must validate inputs and prevent silent propagation of Infinity/NaN values. Silent failures in training loops waste compute and produce invalid models.

title: Intent-to-Agent Routing Fix
rule_summary: Always verify intent-to-agent routing with real user queries. Add classifier feedback loops to detect and correct routing errors.
lesson_summary: Intent classification must be continuously validated against actual outcomes. Routing mismatches cause user frustration and wasted compute cycles.

title: Webview Hydration Recovery
rule_summary: Implement timeout-based hydration recovery in all webview contexts. Never assume initial state sync succeeds.
lesson_summary: Webview/extension communication is unreliable. Always implement recovery mechanisms for missed handshakes and state synchronization.

title: PM2 Process Name Mapping
rule_summary: Create explicit name mapping layers between internal abstractions and external tool naming conventions.
lesson_summary: External tool naming conventions (like PM2) may not match internal abstractions. Always normalize names at integration boundaries.

title: Docker Build Context Dependencies
rule_summary: For monorepo Docker builds: include all workspace package.json files, use --shamefully-hoist, and ensure platform-specific binaries are available.
lesson_summary: Docker builds with monorepos require careful handling of workspace dependencies. pnpm's strict resolution needs explicit configuration in containerized environments.

title: Tailscale SSH Deployment Standard
rule_summary: ALL deployments MUST use Tailscale SSH (100.64.175.88). Never use public IP (104.248.225.250) for SSH.
lesson_summary: Security practices must be enforced at the tooling level, not just documented. Automated systems will fall back to insecure defaults without explicit constraints.

title: CommitDeployLog as Single Source of Truth
rule_summary: ALL agents MUST call CommitDeployLog.recordCommit() after making changes and CommitDeployLog.recordDeploy() when deploying.
lesson_summary: Multi-agent systems require centralized coordination logs. Without them, agents duplicate work and create conflicts.

title: Settings View cachedState Pattern
rule_summary: SettingsView inputs MUST bind to local cachedState, NOT live useExtensionState(). Wire inputs directly to cachedState to prevent race conditions.
lesson_summary: Live state bindings in React can cause race conditions with async updates. Use local cached state for form inputs, only sync on explicit save.

title: SafeWriteJson for Atomic File Operations
rule_summary: MUST use safeWriteJson(filePath, data) from src/utils/safeWriteJson.ts instead of JSON.stringify with file-write operations.
lesson_summary: File writes can fail or corrupt data. Always use atomic write patterns for critical data files.

title: Model Router Service Task Routing
rule_summary: Use the Model Router for all AI calls. Route by task type, not just by user preference. Always have fallback providers configured.
lesson_summary: Different AI providers excel at different task types. A routing layer improves both cost-efficiency and output quality.

title: ML Loop NaN Loss Detection
rule_summary: InfiniteImprovementLoop MUST detect all-NaN loss arrays and stop training with a clear warning. Do not continue training on corrupted models.
lesson_summary: ML training must detect corruption early and stop rather than continuing with invalid data. NaN propagation invalidates all downstream results.

title: Terminal Output Import Path Correction
rule_summary: Always verify imports after moving files. Run build after refactoring to catch path errors.
lesson_summary: Import paths must be verified after refactoring. Automated builds catch these, but only if CI is running.

title: Context Condensation Pattern Preservation
rule_summary: Context condensation MUST preserve function signatures, class declarations, and type definitions. Prioritize by recency with a character budget.
lesson_summary: When condensing context for large conversations, preserve structural information (signatures, types) that the model needs to continue working accurately.

title: Auto-Retry on Empty Assistant Response
rule_summary: Implement auto-retry with exponential backoff for empty API responses. Log each retry attempt.
lesson_summary: APIs can return empty responses due to transient issues. Auto-retry with backoff improves reliability without user intervention.

title: Terminal Output Buffer Memory Leak Fix
rule_summary: All buffers MUST have size limits with automatic cleanup. Never allow unbounded memory growth.
lesson_summary: Long-running processes need bounded buffers. Unbounded growth leads to OOM and performance issues.

title: Test Execution from Correct Directory
rule_summary: Tests MUST be run from the same directory as the package.json that specifies vitest in devDependencies. Run: `cd src && npx vitest run path/to/test-file`
lesson_summary: Monorepos have multiple vitest installations. Tests must run from their local workspace context.

title: Model Warmup for CLI Performance
rule_summary: Warm up AI models on service startup to reduce cold start latency. Handle warmup failures gracefully.
lesson_summary: Cold starts affect user experience. Proactive warmup improves perceived performance.

title: Feat(intelligence): migrate legacy Roo Code lessons and add enhancement system
rule_summary: When migrating legacy code, always document the process and any issues encountered for future reference.
lesson_summary: On May 17, 2026, the legacy Roo Code lessons were successfully migrated to the intelligence layer. The enhancement system was also added during this process. Key insights from the migration included identifying bugs that needed fixing and documenting the lessons learned for future reference.

title: NL routing now passes goal+projectPath so coder intents trigger real coding
rule_summary: Always pass the goal+projectPath along with NL routing data to ensure coder intents are triggered only for real coding sessions
lesson_summary: Previously, the NL routing system was not passing the goal+projectPath to coder intents, causing them to trigger even when the user was not actively coding. This bugfix ensures that coder intents are only triggered when the user is engaged in coding activities, improving the accuracy and relevance of the suggestions provided.

title: Feat: add DeepSeek-V4-Flash and DeepSeek-V4-Pro models with agent re-routing
rule_summary: Always ensure proper configuration and testing when integrating new models or features to maintain system stability and performance.
lesson_summary: This lesson covers the integration of DeepSeek-V4-Flash and DeepSeek-V4-Pro models into the system, along with agent re-routing functionality. The addition of these advanced models enhances the search capabilities and efficiency of the platform.

title: Force shell intent for VPS queries, relax policy to allow non-blocked intents
rule_summary: When processing VPS-related queries, always use the shell intent if not blocked by policy
lesson_summary: The previous policy for handling VPS queries using the shell intent was too strict, blocking many legitimate requests. This fix changes the policy to allow non-blocked intents for VPS-related queries, improving functionality and user experience.

title: Feat: add Memory Explorer — engineering lessons database with search and tag ...
rule_summary: Always document your engineering lessons in a centralized JSONL file for easy searchability and knowledge sharing
lesson_summary: In this engineering lesson, we explore the process of adding a Memory Explorer feature to our dashboard. We'll examine the relevant code files, including page.tsx, sidebar.tsx, and memory-explorer.tsx, as well as the lessons.jsonl file that stores our knowledge base. By following these steps, you'll learn how to effectively implement new features while maintaining a structured engineering process.

title: TOML Duplicate Table Key in Codex Config
rule_summary: Before adding a new `[table]` section to any `.toml` config file, grep for existing instances of that table name and merge keys into the existing section rather than creating a duplicate.
lesson_summary: When adding new configuration keys to TOML files, always check if the table already exists. Appending a duplicate `[table]` header will break parsing.

title: Tighten shell keyword detection, add /shell command, add page labels
rule_summary: When updating shell keyword detection, always add a dedicated /shell command and page labels for clarity and testability
lesson_summary: This bugfix improved shell keyword detection by adding a /shell command. It also added page labels to improve testing and maintainability of the codebase, particularly in the cloud/api/telegram* modules.

title: Parse query params manually for native http server
rule_summary: When building a native HTTP server for the API, always manually parse query parameters using a regular expression to ensure correct handling of incoming requests.
lesson_summary: When building a native HTTP server for the API, we encountered an issue where query parameters were not being parsed correctly. This led to unexpected behavior in our workflow compliance endpoints. By manually parsing the query parameters ourselves using a regular expression, we were able to fix the bug and ensure that the server could properly handle incoming requests with query parameters.

title: Replace Express-style res.json with sendJson for native http server
rule_summary: Always use sendJson instead of res.json when working with a native HTTP server in an Express application.
lesson_summary: Replacing res.json with sendJson when using a native HTTP server in Express can improve performance and reduce overhead. This change helps maintain consistency across the codebase and ensures that all servers are using the same method for sending JSON responses.

title: Add workflow-compliance routes to auth bypass whitelist
rule_summary: Always include workflow-compliance routes in the auth bypass whitelist when they require access without authentication
lesson_summary: In this bugfix, we added workflow-compliance routes to the auth bypass whitelist in auth.js. This ensures that users with valid workflow compliance can bypass authentication for specific routes without compromising security.

title: Update workflow compliance view to use existing UI components
rule_summary: When updating views in the dashboard, always consider reusing existing UI components to improve maintainability and consistency
lesson_summary: The previous implementation of the workflow compliance view in the dashboard used custom components, which made it harder to maintain and update. By refactoring the code to use existing UI components instead, we can ensure consistency across the application and make future updates easier.

title: Feat: add workflow compliance tracking system with DeepSeek delegation
rule_summary: When adding a new feature, always consider how it will be tested and tracked to maintain workflow compliance.
lesson_summary: In this lesson, we will explore the process of integrating a workflow compliance tracking system using DeepSeek delegation. This system helps ensure that automated testing is being performed correctly across various stages of development. By implementing this, teams can catch issues early on and maintain high code quality.

title: Correct terminal output import path
rule_summary: Always verify the import paths used in UI components to ensure they correctly reference the necessary dependencies.
lesson_summary: The correct terminal output import path was not being used in the IDE Terminal component, causing incorrect output to be displayed. This bug was identified and fixed, ensuring that the proper import path is utilized for accurate terminal functionality.

title: Recover webview after missed hydration handshake
rule_summary: When implementing a hydration handshake in a webview UI, always ensure that the handshake is correctly handled and triggered on both client and server sides to prevent missed updates.
lesson_summary: The lesson describes a bug fix for the webview component in a UI system. The core issue was that the hydration handshake was missed, causing the webview to not update properly after certain actions. By modifying the App component and related tests, as well as updating the ExtensionStateContext, the issue was resolved, improving the reliability of the webview UI.

title: Add --no-verify to commit runner, increase timeouts, allow partial apply to p...
rule_summary: Always consider adding flags like --no-verify and adjusting timeouts when optimizing commit runner behavior
lesson_summary: This bugfix involved adding the --no-verify flag to commit runners, increasing timeouts, and allowing partial apply to p. These changes were made to improve the reliability and performance of the system.

title: Notify:status callback now uses correct /telegram/tasks/:id/status route
rule_summary: When updating routes for API endpoints, always verify the endpoint path matches the intended functionality and document any changes made.
lesson_summary: The Notify:status callback was previously using an incorrect route which could lead to failed status notifications. This bugfix update ensures the correct /telegram/tasks/:id/status route is used, improving reliability of status updates sent via Telegram.

title: Add defensive guard in handleMenuCallback, create orchestrator agent, suppres...
rule_summary: Always add defensive guards to API callback functions to validate user permissions
lesson_summary: The lesson describes adding a defensive guard in the handleMenuCallback function to prevent unauthorized access, creating an orchestrator agent for better resource management, and suppressing unnecessary logging output. These changes improve system security, efficiency, and maintainability.

title: Make cloud/api/lib/terminalCore.js self-contained with built-in TerminalBrain
rule_summary: When integrating complex modules, always consider creating a self-contained version to reduce dependencies and improve reliability
lesson_summary: The original design of the TerminalBrain module relied on external dependencies, causing issues with self-containment. By refactoring cloud/api/lib/terminalCore.js to include a built-in TerminalBrain implementation, we eliminated these dependencies and improved stability.

title: Align handleMenuCallback signature with actual caller (telegramBot.js)
rule_summary: When refactoring or updating function signatures, verify the updated signature matches all existing calls and update tests accordingly
lesson_summary: The handleMenuCallback function in telegramBot.js had an unexpected signature that didn't match how it was being called. This mismatch caused unit tests to pass incorrectly, hiding a potential bug. To prevent such issues, always ensure the signatures of exported functions match their actual usage.

title: Add @superroo/terminal-core dependency to cloud/package.json
rule_summary: Always include required dependencies in the cloud/package.json file when integrating new features or fixing bugs
lesson_summary: This lesson demonstrates how to add the @superroo/terminal-core dependency to the cloud/package.json file, which is necessary for fixing a bug in the system. By including this dependency, developers can ensure that the terminal core functionality is properly integrated and functioning correctly.

title: Intent-to-agent routing bug - 'route to coder agent' was routed to debugger a...
rule_summary: When implementing intent-to-agent routing, always ensure each agent is correctly configured to handle its intended types of intents, and validate routing logic through thorough testing and log analysis
lesson_summary: A bug in the intent-to-agent routing logic caused 'route to coder agent' intents to be incorrectly routed to the debugger agent. This was discovered through monitoring logs and analysis, highlighting the importance of vigilant logging and regular code reviews. Fixing this issue improved the accuracy and efficiency of the system's response to user inputs.

title: Wire up debug team end-to-end flow with proper notifications
rule_summary: When implementing end-to-end flows with notifications, always verify that API calls are correctly made to trigger the desired alerts.
lesson_summary: The debug team end-to-end flow was not working properly due to missing notifications. The issue was traced to incorrect API calls in the telegramBot.js file, which prevented proper communication between the bot and the job runner. By fixing these API issues, we ensured that the debug team would receive timely notifications when needed.

title: Add Ollama fallback provider, classifier feedback, and improved askAI error m...
rule_summary: When updating AI-powered chatbots, always consider adding fallback providers, incorporating feedback loops, and improving error handling for enhanced user experience and maintainability.
lesson_summary: This lesson covers the process of adding an Ollama fallback provider, classifier feedback, and improved error handling in askAI's Telegram bot. The key insights include understanding how to integrate a language model fallback mechanism, utilizing classifier feedback for better results, and implementing more informative error messages to aid troubleshooting.

title: Make TelegramOrchestratorBridge.createTask() async so .catch() works
rule_summary: When modifying existing functions, always consider their return type and ensure they return a Promise if they are asynchronous
lesson_summary: The original implementation of TelegramOrchestratorBridge.createTask() was synchronous, causing subsequent .catch() calls to be ignored. By making the function asynchronous and returning a Promise, we ensured that errors could be properly caught and handled.

title: Classifier now routes 'fix' requests to coder instead of debug_plan
rule_summary: When implementing a new feature or changing existing functionality, thoroughly test all edge cases to ensure proper behavior and avoid unintended consequences.
lesson_summary: The classifier was incorrectly routing 'fix' requests to the debug_plan system instead of the intended coder. This bug was identified and fixed, improving the efficiency and accuracy of request handling in the API.

title: Read_logs intent now replies with AI-analyzed natural language instead of raw...
rule_summary: When updating intents in a chatbot API, always ensure the response format is optimized for users, using AI to generate natural language summaries where possible.
lesson_summary: The Read_logs intent in the Telegram bot API has been updated to provide AI-generated natural language responses instead of raw log data. This improves user experience by delivering more human-readable insights from logs, making it easier for users to understand what's happening with their systems.

title: Strip box-drawing chars from pm2 show log path regex
rule_summary: Always validate and sanitize user input or external data before using it in regular expressions or any other part of your code.
lesson_summary: This bugfix removes box-drawing characters from the pm2 show log path regex in tgEndpoints.js, preventing issues with special characters. It ensures consistent behavior and improves reliability of the API endpoint.

title: Use tail -n syntax instead of tail -NUM for BusyBox compatibility
rule_summary: Always use tail -n syntax instead of tail -NUM to maintain compatibility with BusyBox and avoid potential issues in embedded Linux environments.
lesson_summary: Using the tail -n syntax instead of tail -NUM ensures compatibility with BusyBox, a compact utility for embedded GNU/Linux systems. This change prevents issues when using BusyBox-based systems where the tail command may behave differently than on standard Linux distributions.

title: Docs: record NLP coder routing fix in CommitDeployLog
rule_summary: Always record NLP coder routing fixes in the CommitDeployLog
lesson_summary: When fixing an NLP coder routing issue, always record the details in the CommitDeployLog to ensure proper documentation and traceability for future reference.

title: Route natural language coding requests to coder agent instead of debugger
rule_summary: When implementing new features or fixing bugs, always verify that natural language coding requests are being correctly routed to the appropriate agent.
lesson_summary: In this incident, we accidentally routed natural language coding requests to the debugger instead of the coder agent. This caused confusion and frustration for users who expected help with their code but received error messages. The root cause was a misconfiguration in the API that needed to be corrected to properly route requests.

title: Map short PM2 names (api, worker, dashboard) to superroo- prefixed names in r...
rule_summary: Always use consistent naming conventions for different components of a system, mapping short names to their full equivalents where needed.
lesson_summary: This bug fix involved mapping the short names 'api', 'worker', and 'dashboard' to their corresponding 'superroo-' prefixed names in the r... codebase. This was necessary to ensure consistency and clarity throughout the system, preventing potential confusion and errors.

title: Avoid duplicate shared telegram history writes
rule_summary: Always use locks to serialize write operations on shared resources in a distributed system to prevent data inconsistencies like duplicates.
lesson_summary: In a distributed system using shared Telegram history writes, we encountered duplicate entries when multiple nodes wrote simultaneously. The solution involved implementing a locking mechanism to serialize the writes, ensuring data consistency across nodes.

title: Feat: add conversational history system with Telegram integration
rule_summary: When integrating external APIs like Telegram, always ensure proper error handling and fallback mechanisms to maintain system stability.
lesson_summary: In this lesson, we will explore how to integrate a conversational history system with Telegram for enhanced communication capabilities. We'll cover the challenges encountered during testing and learn best practices for seamless API integration.

title: Add auth token to Quick Actions fetch calls in overview dashboard
rule_summary: Always include an auth token in Quick Actions fetch calls for the overview dashboard
lesson_summary: The previous implementation of the Quick Actions fetch calls in the overview dashboard was missing an auth token, which caused issues with data retrieval. Adding the necessary authentication token resolved the bug and ensured proper access to required resources.

title: Wire overview dashboard Quick Actions and fix orchestrator route query params
rule_summary: Always validate and sanitize all incoming query parameters in your API endpoints to ensure they meet expected formats and values.
lesson_summary: The Wire overview dashboard Quick Actions feature was not properly handling the fix orchestrator route query params, leading to incorrect behavior. This lesson explains how the bug occurred and the steps taken to fix it, emphasizing the importance of thoroughly testing API endpoints and validating input.

title: Export splitLongMessage and set global.\_\_redisClient for Telegram mapping hea...
rule_summary: Always export utility functions used by multiple modules and set global variables for shared resources at the beginning of each module.
lesson_summary: This bug fix involved exporting the splitLongMessage function from api.js and setting global.__redisClient in telegramBot.js. This allowed proper handling of long messages in the Telegram API integration and ensured consistent Redis client usage across the application.

title: Add Central Brain proxy route for /brain/\* requests (Cloud IDE terminal)
rule_summary: Always use a Central Brain proxy route for /brain/* requests in the Cloud IDE terminal to ensure proper handling of brain-related API endpoints.
lesson_summary: This lesson explains how to add a Central Brain proxy route for /brain/* requests in the Cloud IDE terminal. This bugfix was necessary because certain API endpoints were not being properly handled, leading to issues with brain-related functionality. By implementing this solution, developers can ensure that all relevant requests are correctly routed and processed.

title: Extend session TTL to 24h and improve readLogs error handling
rule_summary: When encountering persistent errors in logging, always investigate the session timeout settings and consider increasing the TTL if applicable.
lesson_summary: The lesson covers extending the session TTL to 24 hours in the Telegram bot API, which resolved issues with readLogs errors. This improvement enhances the stability and reliability of the bot's functionality.

title: Wire Telegram coding flow end-to-end with Docker sandbox isolation
rule_summary: When implementing complex integrations, always use Docker sandbox isolation to test interactions between components
lesson_summary: The team successfully implemented Docker sandbox isolation to wire the Telegram coding flow end-to-end. This bugfix resolved issues with inconsistent notifications and improved reliability by ensuring seamless communication between the API, bot, notifier, and agent runners. The solution enhances user experience and trust in the platform.

title: Move secrets from ecosystem.config.js to .env file (env_file: ./.\_env)
rule_summary: Always store secrets like API keys and database passwords in a separate .env file, never directly in ecosystem.config.js or any other configuration file.
lesson_summary: Moving secrets from ecosystem.config.js to a separate .env file improves security by reducing the risk of exposing sensitive information. This change helps maintain a clean configuration file and ensures that API keys, database passwords, and other secrets are stored securely in an environment-specific file.

title: Bypass auth for /telegram/mapping endpoint
rule_summary: Always validate user permissions before allowing access to sensitive endpoints
lesson_summary: The /telegram/mapping endpoint required bypassing auth due to a logic error in the authentication system. This allowed unauthorized access, necessitating a fix to maintain security.

rule_summary: Always implement checkWebhook and checkCommand functions in the telegramRateLimiter module when updating the API for compatibility with webhooks and commands.

This bugfix addresses issues where the rate limiter was not properly handling these features, leading to potential security vulnerabilities or incorrect behavior.

title: Feat: telegram response fixes (rate limiter, typing indicators, error message...

The team implemented fixes in key files to ensure proper functionality and user experience. These changes were crucial for maintaining a smooth communication flow between users and the bot.

This bug fix addressed these issues by adding a dedicated login nav tab and easily accessible sign out button in the header, improving user experience and enhancing website security.

lesson_summary: This lesson teaches how to add a login tab with server-side token verification and include a logout button in the dashboard. It addresses an issue where users could not properly log out or verify their authentication status, leading to potential security vulnerabilities.

lesson_summary: This lesson covers a critical bug in the Telegram integration where the chatId 0 caused field mismatches and stubbed WebSocket endpoints. The issue was discovered and fixed, improving the reliability of the Telegram API communication.

title: Wire all mock dashboard views to real APIs + PM2 stability

lesson_summary: This bugfix involved wire all mock dashboard views to real APIs to improve the stability of PM2. By connecting the mock views to actual data sources, we were able to ensure that the dashboard was displaying accurate and up-to-date information. This improvement is important for maintaining the reliability and trustworthiness of our monitoring system.

This bugfix corrected the issue by updating the relevant code, ensuring proper functionality and compatibility.

lesson_summary: In this refactor lesson, we decomposed the large 1808-line component into smaller, reusable hooks. This made the codebase more maintainable and easier to test. The IDE Terminal feature now has cleaner separation of concerns and improved testability.

rule_summary: Always use lazy-loading for critical modules to avoid crashes when dependencies are missing
lesson_summary: This bugfix lesson teaches how to prevent crashes caused by missing modules in the Telegram bot API. By lazy-loading the telegramAgentManager module, we ensure that the bot remains stable even if required dependencies are not available.

title: Consolidate safeJsonParse into shared utility + update autonomous reports

By creating a centralized JSON parsing utility, we can reduce duplication and potential bugs across our system.

title: Use --shamefully-hoist in Docker pnpm install to resolve transitive deps
rule_summary: When using Docker with pnpm install, always include the --shamefully-hoist flag to ensure all transitive dependencies are resolved and included in the container.
lesson_summary: The --shamefully-hoist flag in pnpm can be used with Docker's install command to resolve transitive dependencies, avoiding issues where some packages are not installed correctly. This bugfix ensures that all required dependencies are properly included in the containerized environment.

title: Add pg transitive deps explicitly to memory-core package.json for pnpm strict...
rule_summary: Always explicitly declare transitive dependencies in the package.json file for each package when using pnpm and strict mode.
lesson_summary: This bugfix lesson teaches how to explicitly add transitive dependencies in the pg package to the memory-core package.json file when using pnpm with strict mode enabled. This ensures that all required dependencies are properly declared, preventing issues during installation or deployment.

title: Remove --no-optional flag so esbuild linux binary is installed
rule_summary: Always ensure all optional flags are removed during the installation process to avoid compatibility issues with dependent libraries or tools
lesson_summary: The --no-optional flag was preventing the installation of the esbuild linux binary, causing issues with building projects that relied on it. Removing this flag allowed for proper installation and resolved the build problems.

title: Add --ignore-scripts to Docker pnpm install to skip bootstrap
rule_summary: Always add --ignore-scripts when installing packages with pnpm inside a Docker container
lesson_summary: When using pnpm with Docker, adding the --ignore-scripts flag to the install command in your Dockerfile prevents the execution of scripts during package installation, which can help avoid issues caused by those scripts.

title: Add missing workspace package.json files to Docker build context
rule_summary: Always add all workspace package.json files to the Docker build context when creating a multi-service Dockerfile

rule_summary: When updating Memory Evolution, always include versioning, enable feedback loops, track trust and confidence, and innovate with pgvector-powered features.

title: Central Brain v2.0 improvements — rate limiting, pagination, dedup, project r...
rule_summary: When implementing system upgrades, always consider the impact on performance, organization, and maintainability.
lesson_summary: The Central Brain v2.0 improvements focused on rate limiting to prevent system overload, pagination for better data organization, deduplication to remove duplicates and improve efficiency, and project restructuring for enhanced maintainability and scalability.

title: Overview dashboards should summarize canonical live sources, not parallel mock state
rule_summary: For dashboard overview surfaces, derive summaries from existing canonical endpoints, prioritize exceptions and next actions, and avoid hard-coded operational metrics once live sources exist.
lesson_summary: Overview pages earn trust when they compose canonical live sources into decisions and next actions instead of mixing a few live values with hard-coded operational panels.

title: Dashboard overview data belongs behind one canonical summary contract
rule_summary: For dashboard summary pages, prefer one backend summary endpoint per surface; keep derived alerts, activity ordering, and missing-data semantics server-side so every client reads the same truth.
lesson_summary: A dashboard overview should consume one canonical backend summary payload instead of stitching together partial endpoints and duplicating rollup logic in the browser.

title: Repair malformed JSX at the first broken boundary before chasing follow-on parser errors
rule_summary: For JSX parse cascades, inspect the first reported malformed element and nearby unmatched closing tags before making broad edits elsewhere in the file.
lesson_summary: A malformed rollback button and mismatched closing tag caused a JSX parser cascade; fixing the earliest broken boundary restored the file and exposed the next real compiler issue.

title: Reuse existing null guards when rendering derived editor state
rule_summary: For repeated derived-state calculations, copy the existing safe branch structure or extract a helper instead of retyping a looser variant.
lesson_summary: The Monaco toolbar repeated diagnostics logic without the existing filePath guard, causing a nullable argument type error; aligning it with the already-safe path fixed the dashboard type check.

title: Queue dashboards should expose live rollups through one backend summary contract
rule_summary: For dashboard tabs that summarize several related signals, create one canonical summary endpoint and remove decorative placeholder values instead of blending mock and live state in the UI.
lesson_summary: The queue page mixed one live counter request with many hard-coded values; adding a canonical summary endpoint made every visible metric come from real queue, agent, event, and usage data.

title: A missing pnpm package payload can masquerade as a React runtime bug
rule_summary: For startup-time React/Next failures, verify require.resolve, package symlink targets, and actual package contents before assuming a version mismatch.
lesson_summary: The dashboard build failed before app code because the local pnpm store had an empty react@18.3.1 payload; inspecting package contents revealed install corruption rather than a framework incompatibility.

title: Job detail views need persisted logs and specific routes before broad list routes
rule_summary: For REST surfaces with both collection and item routes, declare specific routes before broad matchers, and never synthesize operational logs in the frontend when a durable backend source exists.
lesson_summary: The Jobs tab fabricated detail logs and its broad list route could swallow item routes; moving route order and exposing persisted job logs restored truthful inspection data.

title: Deploy dashboards should summarize recorded facts, not infer missing telemetry
rule_summary: For dashboard health summaries, compute metrics from persisted backend facts and render unavailable states for absent telemetry; never pair a local-only form with copy that implies infrastructure changes were saved.
lesson_summary: The deploy tab fabricated failure reasons and average duration while exposing a non-persistent config editor; a canonical deploy summary endpoint and read-only target panel restored truthful operational data.

title: Audit views should preserve rich history fields instead of flattening them away
rule_summary: When presenting canonical history records, normalize naming differences but preserve semantically important fields like start/end time, duration, environment, and failure cause instead of flattening them into decorative summaries.
lesson_summary: The Commit & Deploy Log adapter discarded fields that already existed in the canonical log; preserving them restored real audit value without inventing new data.

title: Feat: IDE terminal UX improvements - command history, scrollback, clear chat,...

lesson_summary: The IDE terminal UX improvements addressed issues with command history, scrollback, and clearing chat in the Feat project. These changes were necessary to enhance user experience and ensure smooth communication between developers and the API. The bugfix involved modifying several files, including api.js, lsp-bridge.js, AiChatPanel.tsx, and CodeEditor.tsx.

title: Remove UTF-8 BOM from telegram.tsx to fix SWC parse error
rule_summary: Always remove unnecessary UTF-8 BOM characters from TypeScript files before using them with tools like SWC that may have trouble parsing them
lesson_summary: A UTF-8 Byte Order Mark (BOM) in the telegram.tsx file was causing SWC to fail when parsing it. Removing the BOM resolved the issue, allowing SWC to successfully parse the file.

title: Remove UTF-8 BOM from telegram.tsx
rule_summary: Always remove any extraneous characters like UTF-8 BOMs from the beginning of TypeScript or JavaScript files to prevent import errors
lesson_summary: A UTF-8 Byte Order Mark (BOM) was accidentally added to the top of a telegram.tsx file. This caused issues when importing the module, as the BOM character was not valid JavaScript code. The problem was resolved by removing the BOM from the file. Properly handling and avoiding unnecessary characters in source files is important for maintaining clean, error-free code.

title: Repair telegram.tsx JSX structure after prettier mangling
rule_summary: When prettier reformats telegram.tsx, always review and manually adjust the resulting JSX structure to ensure the UI remains functional and matches the original intent
lesson_summary: The original telegram.tsx JSX structure was mangled by prettier formatting, leading to a broken UI component. The fix involved manually restructuring the JSX to match the intended layout after applying prettier formatting rules.

lesson_summary: In a Telegram.tsx component, an alertRules.map callback was missing its closing brace, leading to syntax errors and potential bugs. This example demonstrates the importance of properly closing callbacks in JavaScript to ensure code correctness and maintainability.

title: SWC JSX parse errors can cascade from a single missing brace
summary: A single missing } in a .map() callback caused SWC to report 'Unexpected token div' at the return statement. tsc --noEmit revealed the real error immediately.
lesson_summary: A missing curly brace in a complex JSX component using SWC transpilation led to cascading parse errors, highlighting the importance of proper syntax and structure in large codebases. This issue demonstrates how a small mistake can propagate through the build process, potentially causing significant delays and frustration for developers. Learning to identify and address such errors early on can save time and improve overall code quality.
rule_summary: Always ensure proper syntax and structure when writing complex JSX components to avoid cascading parse errors during SWC transpilation.

To fix this issue, we exported auth.projects.projectPresence from auth.js and used it in api.js to retrieve all project presence data regardless of ownership. This change ensures that the API provides consistent access to project presence across all relevant projects.

title: Test auto-lesson extraction from hook
rule_summary: When updating scripts used in hooks, always test the updated scripts in a controlled environment before deploying them globally.
lesson_summary: The auto-lesson extraction process was failing due to an issue with the post-commit hook. By fixing the hook script, we were able to successfully extract lessons from commit messages again. This ensures that our knowledge base stays up-to-date with new insights and learnings.

title: Global git hook blocked by stale local hooksPath override in superroo-vsix
rule_summary: When installing a global git hook, scan common repo directories for local hooksPath overrides and warn the user. When debugging hook failures, always check git config --local core.hooksPath first.

title: Separate Retryable System Failures from User Clarification
rule_summary: Before chaining promise methods onto an integration helper, verify whether it actually returns a promise; for Telegram workflows, keep clarification and retryable_failure as distinct states with distinct copy and recovery actions.
lesson_summary: Telegram coding tasks were throwing because a synchronous bridge method was treated like a promise, and retryable model failures were mislabeled as user clarification. Fixed by using try/catch for the sync bridge path and adding a separate retryable failure flow that preserves retry context.

title: Claude Code PostToolUse hook — add retry queue fallback when Central Brain sync fails
rule_summary: When adding a PostToolUse hook or any background sync mechanism that calls a remote API, always add a fallback that writes to ~/.superroo/retry-queue.json on failure.
lesson_summary: The Claude hook's background worker called sync-lessons-to-central-brain.mjs which POSTs to the cloud API. If the API is unreachable, the sync failed silently. Fixed by adding an enqueueRetry() helper that writes to ~/.superroo/retry-queue.json so superroo-learn retry can pick it up later.

title: Add @monaco-editor/react to dashboard deps for Docker build
rule_summary: Always add external dependencies used by Docker builds directly in the project's package.json, not just in local development.
lesson_summary: The lesson teaches how to properly add a dependency in the dashboard package.json for use in Docker builds. This prevents missing dependencies causing build failures. It's crucial for maintaining reliable CI/CD pipelines.

This broke the automated synchronization of lessons data across systems. The fix involved removing the auth middleware from the cloud API to allow unauthenticated access to this specific endpoint.

title: Docker bind-mount cloud/node_modules from host, remove npm install from API/M...
rule_summary: Always use bind-mounts for directories containing node_modules in your Dockerized Node.js applications, to prevent running npm install inside containers during build
lesson_summary: This lesson demonstrates how to properly configure Docker bind-mounts for the cloud/node_modules directory in a Node.js application. By binding-mounting this directory from the host, you can avoid running npm install inside your Docker containers during the build process. This improves performance and reduces the risk of errors. The key takeaway is that using bind-mounts allows you to keep package installations separate from your container builds.

title: Bind-mount root node_modules into Docker API and Mini-IDE containers
rule_summary: Always bind-mount required directories like node_modules into Docker containers when building projects that rely on external dependencies
lesson_summary: The root node_modules directory was not being bind-mounted into Docker API and Mini-IDE containers, causing build errors when trying to install dependencies. This was fixed by adding the necessary volume bindings in the relevant docker-compose.yml files. Properly bind-mounting directories ensures container builds can access external dependencies.

title: Copy node_modules from builder to runtime in dashboard Dockerfile
rule_summary: When building a multi-stage Docker image for a web application with a serverless function and a separate dashboard, always ensure that the builder stage copies the full node_modules directory into each Dockerfile, so both components have access to required JavaScript dependencies at runtime.
lesson_summary: The dashboard Docker image was failing to run due to missing dependencies in the node_modules folder. The issue arose because the builder stage did not copy over the node_modules from the serverless function's Dockerfile to the dashboard's Dockerfile. This caused the dashboard to crash when trying to access its frontend JavaScript code that relied on those modules. To fix it, we added a COPY instruction in the dashboard Dockerfile to explicitly copy the node_modules folder from the builder stage, ensuring all dependencies are available at runtime.

title: Dashboard Dockerfile single-stage build to avoid standalone node_modules conf...
rule_summary: Always use a single-stage Dockerfile when possible to minimize redundant dependencies and optimize build performance.
lesson_summary: The previous approach of building a dashboard Docker image in multiple stages led to unnecessary duplication of node_modules in each stage. This increased build time and disk space usage. By switching to a single-stage build, we can avoid this redundancy and streamline the Dockerfile process.

title: DeepSeek V4 Coder MCP — enforce agent routing workflow for Claude Code
rule_summary: When designing agent routing workflows for Claude Code, do NOT rely on CLAUDE.md instructions alone. Create MCP servers that expose delegated capabilities as tools — Claude auto-discovers them from .mcp.json and uses them when appropriate, making the workflow programmatically enforceable.
lesson_summary: CLAUDE.md is advisory text and cannot enforce agent routing. To make Claude follow a multi-model workflow (Claude plans/reviews → DeepSeek codes → Ollama summarizes), provide MCP tools that Claude can programmatically call. The MCP protocol (JSON-RPC over stdio) is the correct mechanism for Claude to delegate tasks to other models/APIs.

title: Separate missing workflow telemetry from failed compliance
rule_summary: For diagnostic dashboards, model missing, untracked, and failed as separate states, and make deploy health checks cover every endpoint the operator relies on.
lesson_summary: Operational dashboards stay trustworthy when they distinguish absent telemetry from negative outcomes and expose the health of every dependency that can invalidate the page.

title: Set HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 for Dashboard Docker healthcheck
rule_summary: Always set HOSTNAME to 0.0.0.0 in Docker Compose when deploying the dashboard to ensure compatibility with the expected hostname format during health checks.
lesson_summary: The HOSTNAME environment variable was set to the container's hostname by default in the Docker Compose configuration. This caused issues with the dashboard health checks, as they expected a different format for the hostname. By setting HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0, the issue was resolved and the health checks passed successfully.

rule_summary: Always initialize the orchestrator.hermesClaw.bugKnowledgeStore before using it in synchronous functions to avoid uninitialized data access errors.
lesson_summary: In this bug fix, we learned that the orchestrator.hermesClaw.bugKnowledgeStore should be initialized before using it in sync functions. This helps prevent issues with accessing uninitialized data and ensures consistent behavior across different environments.

title: Mini-IDE healthcheck check root path instead of /health
rule_summary: Always configure healthchecks to use a dedicated endpoint like /health, never use the root path for sensitive operations
lesson_summary: The Mini-IDE healthcheck was configured to use the root path instead of /health, which could allow unauthorized access. This bug was discovered and fixed to ensure proper security checks are in place.

title: Ollama MCP Server - fix ESM require() bug and verify VPS connectivity
lesson_summary: This lesson describes the process of fixing a bug in the Ollama MCP Server's ESM require() functionality and verifying connectivity to the VPS. The core insight is understanding how the bug manifested and the steps needed to resolve it, while also learning best practices for maintaining server connectivity. This matters because addressing bugs ensures smooth operation and reliable communication with remote systems.
rule_summary: When encountering a bug in ESM require() functionality within an MCP Server, always investigate the specific error message and trace its origin to the relevant code file. Never assume the issue is unrelated or fix it without proper testing.

title: E2E MCP workflow tests + compliance monitoring for Claude Code
rule_summary: E2E tests for MCP servers MUST account for JSON-RPC 2.0 response structure and use result?.result?.content || result?.content for robust content extraction. DEEPSEEK_API_KEY checks should be warnings, not failures, when the key is configured in .mcp.json env vars rather than the terminal environment.
lesson_summary: Created comprehensive 29-test E2E suite verifying the entire MCP-based workflow is properly wired for Claude Code. Updated the workflow compliance checker with --mcp-check and --all flags to also verify MCP server configuration and connectivity.

title: MCP Codex Bridge - extend DeepSeek + Ollama MCP workflow to Codex VS Code extension
lesson_summary: The core issue was that the MCP Codex Bridge workflow needed to be extended to integrate seamlessly with the Codex VS Code extension, leveraging the capabilities of DeepSeek and Ollama for enhanced functionality.
rule_summary: When extending the MCP Codex Bridge workflow to the Codex VS Code extension, always integrate DeepSeek and Ollama capabilities by creating a new combined script and updating the relevant configuration files.

title: Ollama summarization added to build-agent-context.mjs before planning
rule_summary: When implementing multi-model orchestration, always add graceful degradation: if the cheap local model (Ollama) is unavailable, the expensive model should still get the raw context. Never make the pipeline dependent on a local-only service.
lesson_summary: Added Ollama-powered summarization of lessons, source files, working tree, and bug memory to build-agent-context.mjs. The script now compresses context before feeding it to expensive coding models, with graceful fallback when Ollama is offline.

title: Repo-wide Ollama audit - replaced stale model refs and fetch() calls with curl helper
rule_summary: After changing a model name or connectivity pattern, run findstr /sni old-model-name * across the entire repo and update every match. Also search for the old API pattern (e.g., fetch.*ollama.*api/generate) to catch scripts that need the curl helper.
lesson_summary: Systematic repo-wide audit found 9 files with stale Ollama model references or fetch() calls to Tailscale IPs. Fixed all to use curl-based helper and qwen2.5:0.5b model.

title: Keep Ollama integration aligned with live model inventory
rule_summary: Ollama integrations MUST use live /api/tags inventory for defaults and MUST support both modern /api/embed and legacy /api/embeddings response shapes when embeddings feed learning or RAG systems.
lesson_summary: After the VPS Ollama update to 0.24.0, SuperRoo defaults were aligned to qwen2.5:0.5b with qwen2.5:1.5b fallback, embedding calls were normalized across modern and legacy endpoints, and the Ollama MCP ESM startup bug was fixed.

title: Multi-pass Ollama summarization - chain 2-3 passes per section to make 0.5B model smarter without upgrading
rule_summary: When constrained to a small local model (0.5B parameters), use multi-pass chaining instead of upgrading hardware. Each pass should be a simpler sub-task within the model's capability. Always provide graceful fallback (pass3→pass2→pass1→single-pass→null) so the system degrades gracefully if any intermediate step fails.
lesson_summary: A small 0.5B model can produce useful structured summaries through multi-pass chaining. Each pass stays within the model's limited context window (short input, short output), but the chaining produces better results than a single pass. The key insight: break the task into sub-tasks the model CAN do (extract → condense → format) rather than asking it to do everything at once.

title: Single-pass Ollama summarization beats multi-pass for 0.5B model — A/B comparison with raw context
rule_summary: When using very small local models (≤1.5B parameters) for text summarization, prefer single-pass over multi-pass chaining. Multi-pass only helps when the model has sufficient capacity (7B+) to process and retain information across passes. Always A/B test summarization quality against the raw (no summarization) baseline before committing to a strategy.
lesson_summary: Multi-pass chaining with a 0.5B model amplifies errors because each pass compresses further and the model's tiny context window (~2K tokens) loses information. Single-pass sees the original text once and produces a more faithful compression. The raw (no Ollama) version preserves all details; the Ollama version trades detail for brevity.

title: Swap Ollama to DeepSeek API for context summarization
rule_summary: For context summarization in agent workflows, prefer a cloud API (DeepSeek, OpenAI) over local models smaller than 3B parameters. Add a lightweight .env loader (no npm dependency) for API keys. Always include graceful degradation (skip if API key missing).
lesson_summary: Ollama 0.5B produced poor-quality summaries (hallucinated bugs, truncated text). Swapping to DeepSeek API via standard HTTPS fetch() produced accurate, detailed summaries for all 6 phases. Added loadEnvFile() to read .env without dotenv dependency. DeepSeek API costs (~$0.14/M tokens) are negligible compared to Claude tokens saved by pre-compressed context.

title: Cross-project loadEnvFile() — superroo2 .env as fallback for DEEPSEEK_API_KEY
rule_summary: For any globally-installed CLI tool that needs project-specific secrets: add explicit fallback paths to known project .env files using os.homedir() + relative path, not just __dirname-relative paths. Test from a completely different directory to verify the fallback works.
lesson_summary: Fixed cross-project DeepSeek summarization in superroo-learn.mjs. The loadEnvFile() function used __dirname to find .env, which resolves to ~/.superroo/ when running from the global git hook. Added ~/superroo/superroo2/.env as an additional candidate path so DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is found from any project directory.

title: DeepSeek MCP .env loading and async stdio shutdown
rule_summary: When wiring mandatory model delegation through MCP, test the exact bridge command and the raw MCP stdio server. Load credentials from every supported runtime source and make compliance checks validate the same sources.
lesson_summary: Connected the DeepSeek MCP coding route by loading DEEPSEEK_API_KEY from repo .env in both the MCP server and Codex bridge. Fixed the MCP server's stdin shutdown race by waiting for in-flight async requests, then verified DeepSeek status/code and Ollama 768-dimensional embeddings through the bridge.

title: Global Codex DeepSeek bridge for cross-project coding
rule_summary: When a model workflow must apply to every project, install a global wrapper and update the global agent config. Then validate from a different project directory, not from the source repo.
lesson_summary: Made the Codex extension inherit the SuperRoo model-routing workflow globally by updating ~/.codex/config.toml, ~/.codex/AGENTS.md, and installing ~/.superroo/bin/superroo-codex-bridge wrappers. Verified DeepSeek status/code and Ollama status/embed from quotation-automation-system.

title: Codex must verify global hook status after its own commits
rule_summary: After an agent runs git commit, always verify the hook using a read-only status command that checks log output, retry queue state, and local hooksPath blockers before reporting lesson capture status.
lesson_summary: Implemented a global SuperRoo hook verifier and persistent Codex instructions requiring superroo-verify-hook.cmd --sha <commit-sha> after Codex runs git commit. The verifier reports stored, queued, triggered, blocked, failure, or unknown based on hook logs, retry queue, and local/global hooksPath settings.

title: Hardcoded API keys in client-side code are a critical security vulnerability
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: All API keys and secrets MUST be stored in server-side .env files and accessed through backend proxy endpoints. Client-side code MUST NEVER contain hardcoded credentials. Use a pre-commit hook or linter to detect hardcoded key patterns.
lesson_summary: API keys for fal.ai, Stability AI, Gemini, and DeepSeek were hardcoded in public/client-side JavaScript files. Anyone inspecting the page source could extract and abuse these keys, leading to unauthorized API usage and potential billing charges.

title: Durable fal.ai queue-based rendering for browser tab survival
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: When integrating AI image/video generation APIs, prefer queue-based endpoints over synchronous ones. Implement polling with exponential backoff and store request IDs for resume capability.
lesson_summary: fal.ai synchronous rendering (fal.run) fails when the browser tab is closed or the page is refreshed mid-generation. The rendering job is lost and must be restarted from scratch. Migrated to fal.ai queue-based API (queue.fal.run) which persists jobs server-side and survives browser tab closure.

title: AI prompt engineering for product image generation — prevent object hallucination
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: For AI image generation, always use structured prompt templates with explicit negative prompts. Store prompt templates as configuration, not hardcoded strings. A/B test prompt variations to find the most reliable formulation.
lesson_summary: fal.ai and Stability AI models were generating images with hallucinated objects (extra products, wrong colors, missing details) when prompts were too vague or contradictory. Structured prompt templates with explicit negative prompts and style anchors significantly improved output consistency.

title: Gemini API timeout handling — increase timeout and add OpenAI fallback
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: AI API integrations MUST have: (1) configurable timeouts (min 120s), (2) automatic fallback to alternative provider, (3) circuit breaker to prevent repeated failures, (4) detailed error logging with provider name and response time.
lesson_summary: Google Gemini API calls were timing out after the default 30-second timeout, causing image analysis to fail silently. Increased timeout to 120 seconds and implemented automatic fallback to OpenAI GPT-4o when Gemini fails.

title: OLE2 compound document parsing for WPS .et spreadsheet files
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: When handling Chinese office file formats (.et, .wps), check if they use OLE2 compound document format. Use compound-bytestream or similar OLE2 parser. Extract named streams and handle embedded ZIP content.
lesson_summary: Chinese WPS Office .et spreadsheet files use the OLE2 (Compound Binary Document) format, not standard OpenXML (.xlsx). Standard xlsx libraries cannot parse them. Required custom OLE2 parsing to extract embedded ZIP images from the ETCellImageData stream.

title: Deterministic product-to-image pattern matching with scoring system
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: Prefer deterministic pattern matching over AI for structured data matching tasks. Implement multiple matching strategies with weighted scoring. Set confidence thresholds. Only fall back to AI when deterministic score is below threshold.
lesson_summary: AI-based product-to-image matching was unreliable and expensive. Implemented a deterministic scoring system that matches product codes (e.g., "ABC-123") to image filenames using multiple pattern strategies with weighted scores.

title: Batch matching system with candidate filtering to reduce AI API costs
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: For batch AI processing: (1) pre-filter candidates with cheap deterministic methods, (2) apply medium-cost pattern matching, (3) use expensive AI only for edge cases. This 3-stage approach typically reduces AI costs by 80-90%.
lesson_summary: Processing 1000+ products through AI matching was cost-prohibitive ($10-30 per batch). Implemented a batch matching system that first filters candidates using deterministic attributes (category, color, size) before applying expensive AI matching only to ambiguous cases.

title: DeepSeek AI PDF text extraction with chunking and robust JSON parsing
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: When extracting structured data from AI: implement chunking with overlap, use a robust JSON repair function (strip fences, fix commas, escape quotes), add retry with exponential backoff, and validate output against expected schema before accepting.
lesson_summary: Extracting structured product data from PDF catalogs using DeepSeek AI. Required chunking large PDFs into manageable sections, robust JSON parsing to handle malformed AI responses, and retry logic for failed extractions.

title: Google Drive OAuth2 with Service Account JWT fallback for production uploads
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: For Google Drive API: implement dual auth (OAuth2 + Service Account JWT). Cache tokens with TTL. Use service account impersonation for production. Handle token refresh transparently. Log auth method used for debugging.
lesson_summary: Google Drive upload required OAuth2 authentication. Implemented a dual-auth strategy: OAuth2 for development (user grants access via browser) with automatic fallback to Service Account JWT authentication for production/headless environments.

title: E2E testing pattern for AI image generation pipelines
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: For AI pipeline E2E tests: validate response structure, field types, status codes, timing constraints, and error handling. Do NOT test exact output values. Use schema validation libraries (Joi/Zod) for response structure checks.
lesson_summary: Testing AI image generation pipelines is challenging because outputs are non-deterministic (different image each time). Developed an E2E testing pattern that validates the pipeline structure and metadata rather than pixel-perfect output.

title: UI panel toggle ordering — call hideWorkspacePanels() before setting panel visibility
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: UI panel toggle functions MUST call the reset/hide-all function BEFORE setting individual panel visibility. Never set visibility before resetting.
lesson_summary: UI panels were showing/hiding in wrong order because hideWorkspacePanels() was called after setting individual panel visibility. The hide call would override the visibility settings.

title: Hidden file inputs — use opacity:0;width:0;height:0 instead of left:-9999px
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: Hidden file inputs should use opacity:0;width:0;height:0;position:absolute instead of left:-9999px. This ensures the element is truly hidden without layout side effects while remaining functional for click() triggers.
lesson_summary: Using left:-9999px to hide file input elements caused issues with screen readers and form submission in some browsers. The input was technically off-screen but still took up layout space.

title: Dark mode implementation — use data-theme attribute on html element not body className
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: Theme switching (dark/light mode) MUST use data-theme attribute on the <html> element, not className on <body>. CSS variables should be defined under html[data-theme="dark"] to ensure proper inheritance.
lesson_summary: Dark mode CSS variables were not applying correctly when toggled via body className because some CSS selectors target :root or html element specifically.

title: VPS deployment with Caddy reverse proxy and PM2 process management
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: Node.js production deployment MUST include: Caddy reverse proxy with automatic HTTPS, PM2 process manager with memory limits and auto-restart, environment variables in PM2 env files, and a deployment script. Never expose Node.js directly to the internet.
lesson_summary: Deployed the Product Image Studio app to a VPS using Caddy as a reverse proxy (automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt) and PM2 for Node.js process management. Required proper environment variable handling and Caddyfile configuration.

title: Double-rendering protection — checkAlreadyRendered() before re-queuing completed items
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: Implement idempotency guards for all operations that can be triggered multiple times. Use a Set/Map to track completed items. Check before queuing. Provide a force flag for explicit re-execution.
lesson_summary: Image re-rendering was double-queuing items that were already rendered, causing duplicate API calls and wasted credits. Implemented a checkAlreadyRendered() guard that checks the rendered set before adding to the render queue.

title: Cache-busting vs unique URLs for re-rendered images
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: For re-rendered/re-generated images, use unique versioned filenames (e.g., image_v2.jpg) instead of cache-busting query parameters. This ensures reliable cache invalidation across all browsers and CDNs.
lesson_summary: Re-rendered images were showing the old version because browsers cached the image URL. Cache-busting via query parameters (?t=timestamp) was unreliable across browsers.

title: Surgical DOM updates for re-render instead of full panel rebuild
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: DOM updates for individual item changes MUST be surgical — target only the specific element using data attributes or IDs. Never rebuild entire panels/lists for single-item updates. Use loading states per-item, not globally.
lesson_summary: Re-rendering an image was rebuilding the entire results panel, causing flickering and loss of scroll position. Implemented surgical DOM updates that only replace the specific image element.

title: Sequential image-to-row assignment for spreadsheet extraction
model: multiple (fal.ai, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Stability AI)
rule_summary: When extracting images from OLE2-based spreadsheet formats (.et), assign images to rows sequentially based on extraction order. Do not rely on filenames for mapping. Verify assignments using image dimensions as a sanity check.
lesson_summary: When extracting images from WPS .et spreadsheet files, images needed to be assigned to specific rows. The ETCellImageData stream contains images in sequential order matching the spreadsheet row order.
