The Central Brain's query_memory MCP tool returned successfully (HTTP 200, no er

title: PM2 env_block overrides env_file - hardcode vault keys directly in ecosystem.config.js
rule_summary: When configuring PM2 ecosystem.config.js, NEVER use `process.env.X || ""` in the `env` block for critical secrets that are defined in `env_file`. The `env` block takes precedence over `env_file`. Either hardcode the value directly, or ensure the variable is set in the shell environment before `pm2 start`. Always verify with `cat /proc/<pid>/environ | tr '\0' '\n' | grep KEY_NAME` after restart.
lesson_summary: PM2 env_file directive is unreliable. The env block process.env.X patterns override .env values with empty strings. Fixed by hardcoding SUPERROO_VAULT_KEY directly. Also fixed: shutdown handler, classifier prompt, markdown stripping, button URL validation, missing pg module.

title: VPS project isolation ? QAS must not run on SuperRoo VPS
rule_summary: Before service deploy/removal, verify hostname, Tailscale IP, public IP, DNS A record, project path, and port ownership; enforce per-project VPS allowlists.
lesson_summary: A duplicate QAS stack on the SuperRoo VPS bound port 3001 and caused dev.abcx124.xyz to serve the wrong app. QAS belongs on 165.22.110.111 / 100.86.182.7; SuperRoo belongs on 104.248.225.250 / 100.64.175.88.

title: Feat: implement all 4 priority improvements
rule_summary: When implementing priority improvements, always consider the impact on the entire system and break down changes into modular components
lesson_summary: In this lesson, we will cover the implementation of all four priority improvements in a machine learning system. We'll explore how to integrate these changes into the cloud API, dashboard components, and the LRScheduler module. By following best practices for code organization and modular design, we can ensure that our ML system remains maintainable and scalable.

title: ML Engine - add comprehensive tests for schedulers, CNN layers, and checkpoint
rule_summary: When adding tests for ML engine components: always test forward pass output shape AND backward pass gradient shape for every layer; for schedulers, test initial value, intermediate values, boundary conditions, and reset behavior; for checkpoint systems, test save/load round-trip, best-only mode, and error conditions. Run tests from the correct subdirectory: cd src && npx vitest run super-roo/ml/engine/__tests__/<test-file>.
lesson_summary: Added comprehensive test coverage for CosineAnnealingScheduler (warm restart, cosine curve, reset), DropoutScheduler (linear/exponential/cosine modes, clamping), Conv2D (forward/backward shape, gradient flow), MaxPool2D (forward/backward, stride/padding), Flatten (forward/backward shape), and ModelCheckpoint (saveWithValidation, loadBest, version mismatch, layer count mismatch, clear). All 37 ML Engine tests pass.

title: Healing Module - add ML classification metrics, per-category escalation, repair tracking, and notification routing
rule_summary: When enhancing a healing/self-healing module: always add per-category overrides for escalation thresholds; notification routing should use numeric action levels for reliable comparison; repair attempt tracking enables automatic circuit breaker triggers; trend metrics need a rolling window; confusion matrices are essential for monitoring classifier drift; when testing escalation logic, explicitly pass the full escalationPolicy config.
lesson_summary: Enhanced the Healing Module with four major improvements: (1) new root cause categories (CIRCUIT_BREAKER, DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE, DATABASE_CONNECTION) with classification patterns and diagnostic steps; (2) ML classification metrics with trend tracking, confusion matrix, precision/recall/F1; (3) repair tracking with RepairAttempt interface, repair history, per-category success rate, automatic circuit breaker; (4) per-category escalation with categoryThresholds/categoryActions overrides and notification routing. All 37 SelfHealingLoop tests and all HealingMetrics tests pass.

title: Feat: comprehensive improvements across ML Engine, Healing Module, VPS stability, docs, and Central Brain sync
rule_summary: After extending a shared union type, search for ALL Record<UnionType, ...> maps and switch statements that exhaustively match on that type, and update them before committing. Run tsc --noEmit to catch these errors early.
lesson_summary: Comprehensive improvements across ML Engine (CosineAnnealingScheduler, DropoutScheduler, Conv2D, MaxPool2D, Flatten, ModelCheckpoint tests), Healing Module (repair tracking, per-category escalation, notification routing, circuit breaker), VPS stability (Docker/PM2 port conflict resolution), Central Brain sync (200/239 lessons), and documentation updates.

title: ML Engine — Comprehensive test coverage for schedulers, CNN layers, and checkpoint
rule_summary: For every new ML component (scheduler, layer, optimizer), create a corresponding test file with at minimum: constructor/initial state test, forward pass test, backward pass/gradient test, edge case test, and reset/state management test.
lesson_summary: Added comprehensive tests for CosineAnnealingScheduler (cosine curve, warm restart, reset), DropoutScheduler (linear, exponential, cosine modes, clamping), Conv2D (forward/backward shape, im2col, gradient flow), MaxPool2D (forward/backward, stride/padding), Flatten (forward/backward shape), and ModelCheckpoint (save/load, optimizer state, saveBestOnly, loadBest, clear, version mismatch, parameter mismatch). All 37 ML Engine tests pass.

title: Healing Module — Repair tracking, per-category escalation, notification routing, and circuit breaker
rule_summary: When building self-healing systems, always include: (1) repair attempt tracking with success/failure per category, (2) configurable per-category escalation thresholds, (3) notification routing to multiple channels, (4) circuit breaker to prevent infinite retry loops. Test all 4 subsystems independently.
lesson_summary: Enhanced the Healing Module with 4 new subsystems: RepairTracker (records attempts, calculates success rates), CategoryEscalation (per-category maxRetries and escalation action overrides), NotificationRouter (routes alerts to Telegram/Slack/Email/Dashboard), CircuitBreaker (opens when repair failure rate exceeds 50% in a 10-attempt window). All 37 SelfHealingLoop tests and 22 HealingMetrics tests pass.

title: VPS — Docker/PM2 port conflict resolution and hybrid deployment architecture
rule_summary: When using hybrid Docker/PM2 deployment: (1) maintain a port allocation table in ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAMS.md, (2) never run Docker containers and PM2 processes on the same port, (3) use pm2 restart for non-Docker services only, (4) after restarting the API process via PM2, expect SSH disconnection and reconnect.
lesson_summary: Fixed VPS stability issues: Dashboard and Mini-IDE were in waiting restart state due to port conflicts between Docker containers and PM2 processes on ports 3001 and 8081. Removed conflicting PM2 processes. Updated ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAMS.md to document the hybrid deployment: Docker containers handle Dashboard (port 3001), Mini-IDE (port 8081), API (port 8787); PM2 on host handles Worker, Auto-Deployer (port 8790), MCP Memory (port 3419).

title: Central Brain — Lesson sync infrastructure with retry queue and systemd timer
rule_summary: When building sync infrastructure: (1) always track sync state per-entry (not just a timestamp), (2) implement retry with exponential backoff (2s base, double, cap at 60s, max 5 attempts), (3) use systemd timers for automated periodic sync, (4) always have a local fallback so the system works offline.
lesson_summary: Synced 200/239 lessons from local storage to Central Brain. 41 lessons failed due to timeout on re-sync of already-synced entries. The sync infrastructure includes sync state tracking via .sync-state.json, retry queue with exponential backoff, systemd timer for hourly sync, and graceful fallback to local storage when Central Brain is unreachable.

title: Documentation — ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAMS.md updated with Docker/PM2 hybrid deployment
rule_summary: After any deployment architecture change, update ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAMS.md within the same PR. The Mermaid diagrams should accurately reflect: (1) which services run in Docker vs PM2, (2) port allocations, (3) data flow between services.
lesson_summary: Updated ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAMS.md with 3 section changes: Section 2 (Healing Module Flow) added RepairTracker, confusion matrix, per-category escalation thresholds, notification routing; Section 3 (ML Engine) added CosineAnnealing and DropoutScheduler to LR Schedulers; Section 6 (Deployment) restructured VPS into Docker Containers and PM2 Processes subgraphs.

title: Type safety — Extending union types requires updating all exhaustive Record maps
rule_summary: After extending any shared union type (especially those used in Record<UnionType, ...> maps or switch statements), run tsc --noEmit to catch exhaustive match violations. Search for all Record<UnionType, ...> patterns in the codebase and update them.
lesson_summary: Fixed TypeScript type error TS2739 in RepairPlanBuilder.ts where two Record<RootCauseCategory, string[]> maps were missing 3 new category entries (CIRCUIT_BREAKER, DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE, DATABASE_CONNECTION). Added the missing entries to both defaultsByCategory and categorySpecific maps with appropriate file paths and repair guidance text.

title: nginx /_next/static/ alias causes white screen after Docker rebuild
rule_summary: Never serve Next.js /_next/static/ via nginx alias when the app runs in Docker. Let the Docker container's Next.js server handle static files directly to avoid build ID mismatch after rebuilds.
lesson_summary: After rebuilding a Next.js Docker image with --no-cache, the new build generates different chunk hashes inside the container. If nginx serves /_next/static/ from the host filesystem via alias, the browser gets 404s for all new chunks, causing client-side hydration to fail and producing a white screen. Fix: remove the nginx /_next/static/ location block so requests pass through to the Docker container's Next.js server.

title: Add missing CIRCUIT_BREAKER, DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE, DATABASE_CONNECTION to Repai...
rule_summary: Always include the CIRCUIT_BREAKER, DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE, and DATABASE_CONNECTION patterns when building repair plans in the RepairPlanBuilder
lesson_summary: The missing CIRCUIT_BREAKER, DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE, and DATABASE_CONNECTION were not being handled properly in the RepairPlanBuilder. This led to issues with circuit breaking, deployments failing, and database connections going unhandled. Fixing this ensures these critical failure scenarios are gracefully managed.

title: Telegram Bot Frictionless Coding & Context Awareness Improvements
rule_summary: Hide manual approval UI in auto-mode; use persistent reply keyboards; pass conversation context to classifiers; bound typing indicators with timeouts; handle new callbacks in both notifier and bot routing.
lesson_summary: Fixed auto-mode UX confusion by hiding approval buttons when auto-chaining, added phase-transition progress messages, persistent reply keyboard, typing indicators, similar/audit buttons, and enhanced classifier with conversation context.

title: Compliance tab learning layer health

title: Feat: add ML Engine + Product Memory dashboard views, wire sidebar nav, add /...
rule_summary: When integrating new features like an ML Engine or dashboard views, always thoroughly test the API and frontend to ensure compatibility and performance.
lesson_summary: In this lesson, we will explore the process of integrating an ML Engine into our system, adding new dashboard views for Product Memory, wiring the sidebar navigation, and making various other improvements to the API. We'll also discuss the challenges encountered during deployment and how they were resolved. Understanding these steps is crucial for ensuring smooth integration of advanced features and maintaining a robust, user-friendly product.

title: Close all 10 RAM orchestrator gaps — dashboard view, alerting/Telegram, Deplo...
rule_summary: Always thoroughly review and update your codebase whenever deploying new features or fixing bugs, ensuring all potential gaps are identified and resolved.
lesson_summary: This bugfix lesson teaches how to identify and close all 10 RAM orchestrator gaps in the dashboard view. It covers setting up alerting via Telegram notifications when these gaps occur. The lesson also includes changes to improve deployment configuration. By addressing these issues, system stability and performance can be significantly enhanced.

title: Remove duplicate module.exports in RAMMonitor.js that was overwriting getSwap...
rule_summary: Always ensure only a single 'module.exports' statement exists for each module to avoid unintentional overwrites
lesson_summary: The RAMMonitor.js module had duplicate 'module.exports' statements that were overwriting the getSwap function. This bug was fixed by removing the duplicate exports, ensuring only one definition of each exported variable or function exists. Properly managing module exports prevents unintended overrides and improves code reliability.

title: Remove Docker depends_on API container and port binding — PM2 handles port 87...
rule_summary: Always remove Docker depends_on API container and port binding when using PM2 to handle application ports
lesson_summary: The Docker depends_on API and port binding can cause issues when used with PM2. By removing these dependencies, the application runs more efficiently without conflicts on port 87.

title: MCP server local_json_fallback silently drops lesson data — withFallback() must detect it
rule_summary: Any withFallback() pattern that proxies through an intermediate server must check the source field of the response, not just catch errors. If the source is local_json_fallback, treat it as a failure and fall through to real local storage + retry queue.
lesson_summary: The MCP server at port 3419 proxies hermes_learn to Central Brain daemon (3417) and REST API (8787), but both were unreachable. The MCP server's _proxyWithFallback fell through to _handleLocalFallback which returns success:true with source:local_json_fallback — the lesson data was silently dropped. Fixed by detecting local_json_fallback in withFallback() and falling through to local storage + retry queue.

title: Detect MCP local_json_fallback in withFallback() so lessons are stored locall...
rule_summary: Always verify if MCP local_json_fallback is enabled before utilizing withFallback() for storing lesson data
lesson_summary: The lesson learned was to always check if the MCP has local JSON fallback enabled before using withFallback() in lessons. This prevents issues where lessons are stored locally instead of on the server, potentially leading to inconsistencies or missing data.

title: Add TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and BOSS_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID to ecosystem.config.js env b...
rule_summary: Always configure required environment variables in ecosystem.config.js when integrating third-party services like Telegram bots
lesson_summary: This lesson teaches how to properly set up the TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and BOSS_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID environment variables in ecosystem.config.js, which was missing, causing issues with the Telegram bot integration. Fixing this ensures the bot can send notifications to the correct chat, improving communication flow.

title: Hermes Claw dashboard — correct field names, auto-load skills/resources, surf...
rule_summary: Always verify field names in new components or resources before integration to prevent auto-load skill/resource failures
lesson_summary: The Hermes Claw dashboard lesson teaches engineers to always verify field names when integrating new components or resources. This prevents errors that can cause the auto-load feature for skills and resources to fail, impacting user experience and productivity.

title: Docs: complete lesson for Telegram P0 fixes and record deploy in commit-deplo...

lesson_summary: The lesson learned from fixing the skills frontmatter, ripgrep universal path, dashboard Windows build, and related issues is the importance of maintaining consistent formatting across skill files and properly configuring global paths for efficient searching. This ensures a smooth development process and improves overall code quality.

title: SKILL.md YAML Frontmatter Must Be Valid
rule_summary: Every SKILL.md file MUST have a ---delimited frontmatter block with name and description keys. Description values containing : MUST be double-quoted.
lesson_summary: Missing or malformed YAML frontmatter in .roo/skills/*/SKILL.md causes VS Code extension startup errors. Always add valid ---delimited blocks and quote colon-containing descriptions.

title: ripgrep Universal Package — Add @vscode/ripgrep-universal path to getBinPath waterfall
rule_summary: Maintain a waterfall of all known ripgrep package paths including @vscode/ripgrep-universal/bin/[platform]-[arch]/. Derive platform as process.platform+'-'+process.arch.
lesson_summary: Modern VS Code ships ripgrep via @vscode/ripgrep-universal with platform subdirectory. getBinPath() must include this in the waterfall or ripgrep fails to resolve on newer installs.

title: Next.js Standalone Build Fails on Windows — Symlinks need Developer Mode
rule_summary: Next.js standalone output uses symlinks. On Windows, guard with process.platform === 'win32' to disable standalone mode and skip prepare-standalone.mjs.
lesson_summary: Next.js output:standalone creates symlinks requiring Windows admin/Developer Mode. Gate both next.config.js and build scripts with process.platform === 'win32' check.

title: Remove babel-plugin-react-compiler from Vite — Experimental plugin breaks webview builds
rule_summary: Do not add babel-plugin-react-compiler to production Vite configs for VS Code extension webviews. Use plain react() without Babel plugins.
lesson_summary: babel-plugin-react-compiler is experimental and breaks complex VS Code webview builds with extension-state bridges. Remove it and use plain react() plugin.

title: Ollama Local-First with VPS Fallback — Prefer 127.0.0.1 before Tailscale VPS
rule_summary: Implement local-first resolution: try 127.0.0.1:11434 first with 1-2s timeout, fall back to 100.64.175.88:11434. Log selected endpoint.
lesson_summary: Hardcoding VPS Ollama URL adds Tailscale dependency and latency. Always try local instance first with short timeout, fall back to VPS. Log the selected endpoint.

title: ClineProvider Webview HTML Debugging — Try/catch and logging for resolveWebviewView
rule_summary: Wrap every webview.html assignment in try/catch with logging: URIs used, HTML length on success, full error+stack on failure. Without this, blank panels are impossible to debug.
lesson_summary: VS Code webview initialization failures show as blank panels with no error. Wrapping HTML generation in try/catch with Output channel logging makes failures visible and debuggable.

title: AGENTS.md Multi-Agent Workflow — Agent workflows supersede MCP, learning layer is mandatory
rule_summary: In multi-agent documentation, separate mandatory rules (learning layer sync, always required) from flexible workflow preferences (coding agent choice, agent-specific).
lesson_summary: Multi-agent architecture docs must explicitly separate mandatory protocol rules (learning layer sync) from flexible workflow defaults (which coding agent to use).

title: MermaidBlock Simplification — Remove Mermaid renderer, fall back to CodeBlock on upstream breakage
rule_summary: When a renderer component causes build failures from upstream breaking changes, immediately fall back to a safe simple representation (code/text) and log as regression.
lesson_summary: Mermaid v11.4.1 module restructuring broke static diagram type imports in the webview. Simplified to 13-line CodeBlock fallback to unblock build. Complex renderer to be restored after upstream stabilizes.

title: Ollama Docker Entrypoint Optimization — env vars for high-memory VPS performance
rule_summary: Set OLLAMA_NUM_THREADS, OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS, OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH, OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1, OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_QUANTIZATION=8bit, OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE using shell-default syntax for overridability.
lesson_summary: Ollama default settings are conservative. For VPS with 16+ cores and large RAM, explicit performance env vars (NUM_THREADS, FLASH_ATTENTION, CONTEXT_LENGTH) dramatically improve throughput.

title: nginx mini-IDE Path Prefix — Strip /tg/ prefix when proxying to mini-IDE
rule_summary: For nginx location /prefix/ blocks proxying to backends without that prefix: always add trailing slash to proxy_pass or use rewrite to strip the prefix.
lesson_summary: nginx forwards full path including location prefix unless explicitly stripped. Mini-IDE expected paths without /tg/. Fixed by stripping prefix in proxy_pass with trailing slash.

title: mini-IDE Terminal — Replace exec with node-pty WebSocket terminal for interactive PTY support
rule_summary: For web-based terminals: always use node-pty (server) + WebSocket + xterm.js (client). Never use exec/spawn without PTY for interactive use.
lesson_summary: exec-based terminals cannot handle interactive programs. Replaced with node-pty + WebSocket + xterm.js to give the mini-IDE a real PTY with full ANSI support and interactive program compatibility.

title: Autonomous Loop Status Guard — Guard JSON.parse against HTML error page responses
rule_summary: All API polling components MUST wrap fetch+JSON.parse in try/catch and check response.ok. Non-JSON responses (nginx error pages) will otherwise crash React components.
lesson_summary: Autonomous loop dashboard component crashed when API returned HTML 502 pages from nginx. Fixed by guarding JSON.parse with try/catch and showing reconnecting state instead.

title: Telegram Bot Missing Brace — Silent logical bug from unmatched orchestratorBridge if-block
rule_summary: After any major refactor adding nested if-blocks in JavaScript, run Prettier or a brace-matching check. Unmatched braces in JS are silent but cause logical errors.
lesson_summary: Missing closing brace for if(orchestratorBridge) caused all Telegram reply logic to be inside that condition. Bot silently sent no responses when orchestratorBridge was null.

title: Telegram Orchestrator submitDirect() — Route all task paths through orchestrator bridge
rule_summary: When adding new Telegram message handler paths, ALWAYS route through orchestratorBridge.submitDirect(), never directly to the API. The bridge provides queue management, rate limiting, and learning layer hooks.
lesson_summary: Multiple Telegram handler paths bypassed the orchestrator, skipping queue management and learning layer. Fixed by adding submitDirect() to TelegramOrchestratorBridge and routing all handlers through it.

title: DeepSeek Model Name Format — V4 API uses deepseek-v4-flash not deepseek-chat-v4-flash
rule_summary: All DeepSeek model names MUST be defined as named constants in cloud/providers/deepseek.js and imported everywhere. V4 format: deepseek-v4-flash, deepseek-v4-pro (no -chat- infix).
lesson_summary: DeepSeek V4 dropped the -chat- infix from model names. Code using deepseek-chat-v4-flash got 404s. Fixed by updating constants in one provider file and importing everywhere.

title: LocalBrain Integration in extension.ts — Instantiate in activate() and inject via constructor
rule_summary: Service instances in VS Code extensions should be instantiated in activate() and injected via constructor. Avoid module-level singletons.
lesson_summary: LocalBrain wired into extension activate() and passed to API constructor for clean dependency injection. Avoids module-level singleton anti-pattern.

title: Learning Layer Multi-Source Sync — All three stores must be updated after adding lessons
rule_summary: After appending lessons to lessons-learned.md, ALWAYS append to lesson-index.jsonl with unique lesson-NNN ID, then run node scripts/ollama-summarize-lesson.mjs.
lesson_summary: SuperRoo learning layer has three parallel stores (md, jsonl, json) each serving different consumers. All must be updated together or lessons are invisible to automated search.

title: Shell Keyword Detection Tightening
rule_summary: Added inferShellCommand() pre-classifier and relaxed canRunWithoutApproval to only block blockedActions
lesson_summary: VPS version queries misclassified as unsafe shell intent, triggering policy block instead of answering

title: SuperRoo uses three parallel learning layer stores
rule_summary: The SuperRoo learning layer maintains three synchronized stores: (1) memory/lessons-learned.md — human-readable markdown, (2) memory/lesson-index.jsonl — structured JSON index for getLessonRetriever(), (3) memory/lesson-summaries.json — AI summaries with embedding metadata.
lesson_summary: The SuperRoo learning layer maintains three synchronized stores: (1) memory/lessons-learned.md — human-readable markdown, (2) memory/lesson-index.jsonl — structured JSON index for getLessonRetriever(), (3) memory/lesson-summaries.json — AI summaries

title: AGENTS.md: agent-specific workflows supersede MCP, but learning layer is mandatory
rule_summary: Clarification added to AGENTS.md: agents may use their preferred coding workflows (Claude Code using its own tools, DeepSeek using MCP, etc.) but MUST contribute to the shared learning layer (wf-004).
lesson_summary: Clarification added to AGENTS.md: agents may use their preferred coding workflows (Claude Code using its own tools, DeepSeek using MCP, etc.) but MUST contribute to the shared learning layer (wf-004). The Central Brain is the master source of truth.

title: Ollama Docker: explicit perf env vars make VPS inference dramatically faster
rule_summary: Ollama defaults are conservative.
lesson_summary: Ollama defaults are conservative. On a 16+ core VPS with large RAM, set: OLLAMA_NUM_THREADS=16, OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=5, OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH=65536, OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1, OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_QUANTIZATION=8bit, OLLAMA_BATCH_SIZE=512, OLLAMA_KEEP_A

title: nginx proxy: trailing slash on proxy_pass strips the location prefix
rule_summary: nginx location /prefix/ { proxy_pass http://backend; } forwards the full path including /prefix/.
lesson_summary: nginx location /prefix/ { proxy_pass http://backend; } forwards the full path including /prefix/. To strip the prefix so the backend sees paths without /prefix/, use a trailing slash: proxy_pass http://backend/. This is required for mini-IDE (/tg/ pr

title: CI/CD pipeline: never skip tests on main branch, always gate deploys on green
rule_summary: Never deploy to production without a passing CI gate. All commits to main must pass lint, type-check, and unit tests before the deploy step runs.
lesson_summary: SuperRoo uses PM2 on VPS with no automated CI gate. Deploys have failed due to TypeScript errors that CI would have caught. A green CI run is the minimum bar before any production deploy.

title: Caching strategy: cache AI embeddings and model outputs to avoid redundant Ollama calls
rule_summary: Cache embeddings in PostgreSQL after first generation — never re-embed the same text twice. Cache Hermes 3 summarizations by content hash for lesson summaries.
lesson_summary: Ollama embedding calls (nomic-embed-text) take 100-300ms each. Embeddings are deterministic so caching by content hash in the DB eliminates redundant Ollama calls entirely.

title: PostgreSQL pgvector: always create HNSW index on embedding column for sub-10ms similarity search
rule_summary: Always create an HNSW index (CREATE INDEX ON table USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops)) on any pgvector embedding column used for similarity search.
lesson_summary: Without an HNSW index, every similarity query scans all rows sequentially (~500ms). With HNSW, query time drops to under 10ms. The index must be created after CREATE EXTENSION vector.

title: Thinker Agent Workflow � Four-layer thinking process for Kilo Code
rule_summary: When creating multi-agent workflows, define explicit roles with clear handoff points. Each agent should know what context it receives and what format to pass to the next agent.
lesson_summary: Created thinker, architect, coder, and reviewer agents with four-step workflow. Added PostgreSQL/pgvector integration to Central Brain MCP server with JSONL fallback. Removed DeepSeek dependency, using Ollama models exclusively.

title: Reviewer Agent � Code review and quality assurance for Kilo Code
rule_summary: Every implementation workflow should include a review step with structured feedback and lesson contribution.
lesson_summary: Created reviewer agent using Auto Free for code review and quality assurance. The reviewer is the final step in the four-layer workflow, ensuring code quality before completion.
